title: phaius wallichii var. assamica
intro: Phaius wallichii var. assamica, commonly known as the 'Assam orchid', is a rare and endangered variety of terrestrial orchid native to the eastern Himalayas and parts of Assam. The Assam orchid has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Phaius wallichii var. assamica include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the Assam orchid can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health issues.
